questions.question: The Taj Mahal was built by which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal in Agra (1632–1653) in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor is known as 'Akbar the Great'?
questions.explanation: Akbar (1556–1605) is regarded as one of the greatest Mughal emperors known for religious tolerance, administrative reforms, and expanding the empire.

questions.question: Which was the first capital of the Mughal Empire in India?
questions.explanation: Agra was the first capital of the Mughal Empire in India. Babur established it after the First Battle of Panipat (1526).

questions.question: Sher Shah Suri introduced the currency called:
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri (1540-1545) introduced the silver rupee (rupiya) weighing 178 grains. He also introduced copper paisa and gold mohur — currency reforms that continued under Mughals.

questions.question: Who was the author of 'Baburnama'?
questions.explanation: Baburnama (Tuzuk-i-Baburi) was written by Babur himself in Chagatai Turkish — it is the first autobiography in Islamic literary tradition, describing his life, campaigns, and observations of India.

questions.question: Which city was called 'City of Victory' after the Battle of Panipat 1526?
questions.explanation: After the First Battle of Panipat, Babur captured Agra and made it his capital. Delhi was also used. The battle didn't create a new city; Babur ruled from existing cities.

questions.question: Ibadat Khana (House of Worship) was established by:
questions.explanation: Akbar established Ibadat Khana (House of Worship) at Fatehpur Sikri in 1575 CE for religious debates among scholars of different faiths — precursor to his Din-i-Ilahi.

questions.question: The Battle of Khanwa (1527) was between:
questions.explanation: Battle of Khanwa (17 March 1527): Babur defeated Rana Sanga (Rana Sangram Singh) of Mewar, who had formed a coalition of Rajput chiefs to challenge Babur.

questions.question: Akbar defeated Hemu in:
questions.explanation: Second Battle of Panipat (5 November 1556): Akbar's general Bairam Khan defeated and killed Hemu (the last Hindu emperor to sit on Delhi's throne briefly), securing Mughal rule.

questions.question: The Mughal emperor who was also called 'Zinda Pir' (Living Saint):
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb was called 'Zinda Pir' (Living Saint) for his piety — he fasted regularly, never drank alcohol, lived simply, and supplemented his income by copying Qurans and making prayer caps.

questions.question: Akbar's 'Sulh-i-Kul' policy means:
questions.explanation: Sulh-i-Kul (Peace with all/Absolute peace) was Akbar's policy of universal tolerance — treating all religions equally, respecting all faiths without discrimination.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor translated the Ramayana and Mahabharata into Persian?
questions.explanation: Akbar had the Ramayana, Mahabharata, and other Sanskrit texts translated into Persian, making them accessible to Persian-speaking scholars and promoting cultural synthesis.

questions.question: Babur founded the Mughal Empire after winning:
questions.explanation: Babur (Zahir-ud-din Muhammad) defeated Ibrahim Lodi at the First Battle of Panipat (21 April 1526) using gunpowder and artillery (tulugma tactics). This established the Mughal Empire. He then defeated Rana Sanga at Khanwa (1527).

questions.question: Sher Shah Suri is famous for:
options: Introducing the Grand Trunk Road, administrative reforms, land revenue system (zabt), and silver rupee
questions.explanation: Sher Shah Suri (1540–1545) defeated Humayun, reorganised the empire: Grand Trunk Road (Peshawar to Bengal), silver rupee (precursor to modern rupee), land revenue based on measurement (zabt), relay postal system, and built Sher Shah mosque.

questions.question: Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi at the First Battle of Panipat in:
questions.explanation: First Battle of Panipat (21 April 1526): Babur defeated Ibrahim Lodi using the Tulughma strategy and artillery, establishing the Mughal Empire in India.

questions.question: The Mughal emperor famous for building Taj Mahal:
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan (1628-1658) built the Taj Mahal at Agra (1632-1643) as a mausoleum for his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal. It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.question: Aurangzeb reimposed the 'Jizya' tax which had been abolished by:
questions.explanation: Akbar abolished the discriminatory Jizya (poll tax on non-Muslims) in 1564 CE as part of his policy of religious tolerance. Aurangzeb reimposed it in 1679 CE.

questions.question: Todar Mal was the Finance Minister of:
questions.explanation: Raja Todar Mal was Akbar's revenue minister who introduced the Zabti (measurement) system of land revenue — measuring land and fixing revenue based on soil type and cultivation.

questions.question: The 'Mansabdari system' was a Mughal system of:
questions.explanation: Mansabdari system (introduced by Akbar) graded military and civil officials (mansabdars) by numerical ranks (mansabs) determining salary and cavalry obligation.

questions.question: The Battle of Haldighati (1576) was fought between:
questions.explanation: Battle of Haldighati (18 June 1576) was fought between Maharana Pratap of Mewar and the Mughal forces led by Man Singh I on Akbar's behalf. Pratap was defeated but never surrendered.

questions.question: The Battle of Buxar (1764) was significant because:
options: British defeated combined armies of three powers
First use of firearms in India
questions.explanation: Battle of Buxar (22 October 1764): British under Hector Munro defeated the combined forces of Mir Qasim (Bengal), Shuja-ud-Daula (Awadh), and Mughal Emperor Shah Alam II — establishing British dominance.

questions.question: The 'Ain-i-Akbari' was written by:
questions.explanation: Ain-i-Akbari (Institutes of Akbar) was written by Abul Fazl, Akbar's court historian. It is the third volume of Akbarnama, providing detailed accounts of Akbar's administration, policies, and the Mughal court.

questions.question: Akbar's 'Ain-i-Akbari' and 'Akbarnama' were written by:
questions.explanation: Abul Fazl (1551–1602), a minister and historian, wrote Akbarnama (in 3 volumes, including the Ain-i-Akbari). The Ain-i-Akbari is a detailed account of Akbar's administration, court, culture, and statistics of the empire.

questions.question: The Mughal land revenue system during Akbar's reign was called:
options: Dahsala or Zabti system (introduced by Todar Mal)
questions.explanation: Raja Todar Mal introduced the Dahsala (ten-year average) system: land was measured (zabt), soil quality classified into 4 types, and revenue fixed for 10 years based on average produce. It replaced earlier arbitrary systems.

questions.question: Shah Jahan built the Taj Mahal in memory of:
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan (1628–1658) built the Taj Mahal at Agra (1631–1648) in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal (Arjumand Banu Begum) who died during childbirth. It was built by 20,000 workers using white Makrana marble.

questions.explanation: Babur founded the Mughal Empire in 1526. He was a Central Asian ruler.

questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the mansabdari system to organize the Mughal army and administration.

questions.explanation: The Mughal administrative system featured zamindari, mansabdari, and jagirdari.

questions.explanation: Bahadur Shah II was the last Mughal emperor, exiled by the British in 1857.

questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the Din-i-Ilahi, a syncretic religion, in 1581.

questions.question: Who was the first Mughal emperor to issue a firman granting trading rights to the British East India Company?
questions.explanation: Jahangir granted trading rights to the British East India Company in 1613.

questions.question: The Mansabdari system was introduced by which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Akbar introduced the Mansabdari system to organize the nobility and army.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor built the city of Fatehpur Sikri?
questions.explanation: Akbar built Fatehpur Sikri to honor Sufi saint Salim Chishti.

questions.question: The Battle of Samugarh (1658) was fought between which two Mughal princes?
questions.explanation: Aurangzeb defeated Dara Shikoh in the Battle of Samugarh to claim the throne.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor abolished the Jizya tax?
questions.explanation: Akbar abolished Jizya in 1564 as part of his liberal religious policies.

questions.question: The Mughal Empire reached its greatest territorial extent under which emperor?
questions.explanation: Under Aurangzeb, the Mughal Empire expanded to its maximum size, covering almost the entire Indian subcontinent.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor introduced the Din-i Ilahi religious order?
questions.explanation: Akbar founded the Din-i Ilahi in 1582, aiming to synthesize elements of various religions.
